source: ACCWS Pilot Testers Google Drive folder (Apr 27 2026)
notes: Static seed data for 9 pilot sites (13 lagoon plans). Snake_case keys + ISO dates so a Laravel seeder/resource can consume this directly. Sundre and Parkland County plans not yet available.

products.name: Sludge Rx
products.description: Pelletized microbial blend for warm months that penetrates sludge and breaks down complex organics. Often paired with OxyPaks XL.

products.name: Polar Rx
products.description: Cold-weather sludge treatment, active down to 1.5 C. Extends treatment season and supports ammonia reduction in cold transitions. Typically paired with VitaStim Polar.

products.name: VitaStim Polar
products.description: Cold-weather microbial blend that maintains biological activity in colder months. Stabilizes ammonia, TSS, and cBOD.

products.name: VitaStim Sludge Reducer
products.description: Warm-season biological. Enhances digestion of solids before sludge formation, supports denser nutrient-sequestering sludge.

products.name: OxyPaks XL
products.description: Granular oxygen-releasing compound that boosts microbial activity in the sludge layer. Loosens sludge surface and helps reduce odours.

products.name: Qwik-Zyme L
products.description: Lipase-rich liquid enzyme that breaks down FOG into bioavailable nutrients. Reduces grease buildup, supports VFA production.

products.name: Qwik-Zyme P
products.description: Protease enzyme that breaks down complex proteins into amino acids. Improves VFA production and supports phosphorus uptake.

products.name: Bug on a Rope
products.description: Slow-dissolving microbial brick for lift stations. Releases grease-eating bacteria and mild oxygen source; controls FOG and H2S.

products.name: GreaseZilla
products.description: Liquid microbial degreaser with concentrated bacteria and biocatalysts for severe FOG issues in lift stations and force mains.

products.name: Nature's Blend
products.description: Dry probiotic for storage cells. Targets soluble nutrients and stimulates biological nutrient removal under carbon-limited conditions.

products.name: Polar Blend
products.description: Cold-season counterpart to Nature's Blend. Boosts floc formation and biological nutrient removal at low temperatures.

products.name: Total Floc
products.description: Biological flocculant and phosphorus binder. Binds and removes phosphorus while reducing BOD, TSS, and total nitrogen.

products.name: Ultrasonic Algae Control (HBS SolaRaft-QDB)
products.description: Suspended ultrasonic transducer that emits 360-degree ultrasonic waves to disrupt algae growth. Stabilizes pH/DO without chemicals.

products.name: Lift Station Aeration
products.description: 120L compressor + 50 ft self-sinking air hose for lift station aeration. Suspends solids, breaks up FOG, reduces odors.

treatment_plans.executive_summary: 15-year-old multi-cell lagoon serving ~1,000 people, never dredged. Currently meeting federal WSER limits for cBOD/TSS/un-ionized ammonia. Both 2024 effluent samples show negative removal for phosphorus and COD (benthic feedback) - lagoon is releasing stored nutrients, signaling approach to sludge capacity. First-year biological optimization to stabilize nitrification, improve sludge digestion, and reduce phosphorus release before WSER exceedance becomes a risk.
objectives: Protect compliance margin for cBOD/TSS/un-ionized ammonia
Reduce internal P and COD release from accumulated sludge
Establish sludge survey baseline (Spring 2026) for long-term dredging decision

monitoring.additional_sampling: Add September event before fall release; June early-season event if feasible

systems.name: Wainwright Lagoon
systems.discharge: continuous to Bushy Head Lake / Battle River
systems.permit_constraint: Dosing restricted to upstream manholes/lift stations only; no direct lagoon dosing

cells.name: Aerated Front Cell
cells.notes: Fully aerated - disrupts anaerobic phase needed for EBPR

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Aerated front cell eliminates the anaerobic step EBPR needs, leaving ~4 mg/L effluent PO4-P. Plan compensates via upstream enzymatic hydrolysis at lift station/manhole (permit-compliant) to generate VFAs that support PAOs.
objectives: Reduce effluent PO4-P from ~4 mg/L toward <=2 mg/L (40-60% reduction target)
Maintain low ammonia and stable BOD/TSS
Reduce odor at industrial lift station wet well
Sludge survey 2025/early 2026 ahead of 2026 dredging

systems.name: Fox Creek Lagoon
systems.discharge: spring + optional fall release; effluent sold to fracking when possible

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Sludge has resurged in anaerobic cells (estimated 25-50% volume) only 5 years after 2019 mechanical dredging. Council wary of another dredge cycle; biological sludge reduction targets Cells 1-4 to reclaim hydraulic capacity and stabilize spring ammonia/BOD spikes.
objectives: Reduce 25-50% sludge occupancy in anaerobic cells
Delay next mechanical dredge by 3+ years
Control spring ammonia rebound and BOD spikes (April 2023 was 19.3 / 53.3 mg/L)

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Effluent currently strong but rapid population growth + I&I increasing hydraulic load. Risk is capacity preservation (sludge accumulation in anaerobic cells) rather than acute effluent failure. Three-tier offering with set-pricing option for budget predictability.
objectives: Protect biological activity in anaerobic cells
Extend dredging interval
Maintain budget discipline aligned with Town's flow meter / I&I priorities

tiers.name: basic
tiers.label: Basic (Sludge Maintenance)
tiers.notes: 3-year set pricing available with CPI cap

sludge_survey.scope: Full baseline year 1; Cells 1-2 tracking after

systems.name: Redwater Lagoon
systems.discharge: bi-annual (typical); spring ammonia control is critical

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Aerated lagoon performs well in warm months but has predictable spring ammonia vulnerability (cold + high pH from algae). 2025 data: April ammonia 5.34 mg/L (unionized ~0.68 mg/L). Plan adds cold-weather bioaugmentation, Cell 1-2 sludge maintenance, year-round upstream Qwik-Zyme L, and optional ultrasonic in storage cell.
objectives: Suppress spring ammonia rebound (target unionized <0.3 mg/L)
Reduce internal phosphorus release from Cell 1-2 sludge
Control summer algae-driven TSS via optional ultrasonic

sludge_survey.scope: All 10 cells baseline; Cells 1-3 tracking after

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Late-summer pH spikes in Cell 6 (e.g., 9.71 in Sep 2024) restrict irrigation reuse. Root cause: algae blooms in shallow Cells 1-4. Plan suppresses algae at end of anaerobic train (Cell 4 ultrasonic) and uses seasonal bioaugmentation to control nutrient release.
objectives: Effluent pH <=8.5 for >=90% of Aug-Sep measurements
Reduce odors at lift stations/headworks
Measured depth gains in Cells 1-4

dosing_schedules.notes: Trigger only if spring P>1 mg/L and ammonia>0.5 mg/L

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Persistent odor driven by spring turnover, algal bloom-crash cycles, high-strength industrial loading (butcher/vet/dairy), and cold-weather nitrification failure. Existing Dazzle Sewer Sweetener masks rather than treats. Plan shifts from masking to active mitigation via Lift Station A enzyme pretreatment + 4 anaerobic cells of seasonal bioaugmentation.
objectives: Reduce odor (eliminate Dazzle dependence)
Reduce wetland inlet ammonia spring peaks (was 23.38 mg/L May 2022)
Continue sludge reduction (Cell D reduced 1000+ m3 2022-2024)

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Strong effluent (Oct 2025: BOD<4, TSS 3.4, ammonia 0.20, P 1.17 mg/L) masks early front-end stress: sludge resurfacing in Cells 1-2 after cooling + collection-system flushing. Stronger protein/fat loads from butcher shop. Plan sustains shoulder-season biology in primary cells before storage cell shows effects.
objectives: Stay ahead of front-end sludge accumulation while effluent is still strong
Sustain cold-weather biology through shoulder seasons
Spring 2026 sludge survey baseline

systems.name: Colinton Lagoon (Athabasca County)
systems.notes: Originally two-cell, now single cell after emergency berm breach. 40% sludge occupancy.

systems.name: Island Lake Lagoon (Athabasca County)
systems.notes: No prior biological treatment. 44% sludge occupancy. Cattails at inlet.

systems.name: Wandering River Lagoon (Athabasca County)
systems.notes: Sept 2025 effluent crashed (ammonia 54.3, TSS 93, P 11.6) after switch to alternative product disrupted nitrifying community.

systems.name: Grassland Lagoon (Athabasca County)
systems.notes: Cell 1 sludge doubled 2020-2024. Cell 2 self-reducing. 2025 ammonia and P spiked without treatment.

systems.name: Rochester Lagoon (Athabasca County)
systems.notes: Evaporative (no discharge). Recently dredged Cells 1-2. Garbage causing level pipe blockages.

treatment_plans.executive_summary: Single-cell (post berm breach) with 7,536 m3 sludge (40% of volume). 24-month trend shows BOD +18%, TSS +35%, ammonia +47%, P 2.17 mg/L. TSS:BOD 3.14 indicates algae-dominant. Inlet/truck-dump zones targeted with OxyPaks, Sludge Rx, Polar Rx. Optional Total Floc for P binding.

Largest capacity constraint in county: 44% sludge volume, 5,931 m3 accumulated, only 7,570 m3 effective water. Cattails at inlet are symptom of sludge platforms. Inlet-focused bioaugmentation to reclaim capacity.

2023-2024 sludge reduced 1,100 m3 (no mechanical dredge). 2025 effluent crashed after switch to alternative product disrupted nitrifying community. Plan rebuilds stable nitrifying biomass while continuing gradual sludge reduction. BEST tier adds 3x Total Floc; GOOD tier uses Polar/Nature's Blend.
Cell 1 sludge 898 m3 (2020) -> 1,950 m3 (2024). Cell 2 self-reducing. 2025 ammonia surged 1,743% (0.07 -> 1.29) and P +152% without treatment. Focus on anaerobic Cell 1.
Evaporative system (no discharge). Recently dredged. Two-week intensive post-dredge schedule plus autumn stabilization. No effluent data due to no discharge.
